# HRS — Humanoid Robot Solutions
> Humanoid robots, deployed for real-world work in UK industry.

HRS (Humanoid Robot Solutions) is a UK humanoid robot integrator. We take humanoid robots from live demonstration to full deployment across manufacturing, warehousing and logistics — proven on your factory floor before you commit, then integrated and trained to work safely alongside your team. HRS is platform-agnostic: we select the right humanoid for each task rather than reselling a single brand.

## Company
- Name: HRS (Humanoid Robot Solutions)
- Website: https://hrsrobot.co.uk
- Contact: info@hrsrobot.co.uk
- Based: United Kingdom (British robotics company)
- Category: Humanoid Robotics / Physical AI / Industrial Automation / Manufacturing

## Founder
- Llewelyn Rees — Founder & CEO. 20+ years in automation and robotics. Founded Reeco in 2016 to pioneer cobot solutions and collaborative-robot integration in the UK, growing it to a market-leading team of 32 serving the food and pharmaceutical industries. Now bringing that operator experience to humanoid robots, with a factory base for live humanoid demonstrations.

## Robots & Platforms (platform-agnostic)
Full specifications, subsystem breakdowns and live footage: https://hrsrobot.co.uk/robots

- AGIBOT G2 — industrial-grade humanoid robot built for factory floors. Dual 7-DOF force-controlled arms (5 kg payload per arm, 10 kg dual-arm lift, ±0.5N force control, sub-millimetre assembly precision). Dual 3D LiDAR for 360° perception. Onboard NVIDIA Jetson T5000 (2070 TFLOPS) plus a Rhino R1 co-processor (500 TOPS). Height up to 180 cm (adjustable), 185 kg, 26 degrees of freedom, 1.5 m/s omnidirectional wheeled base, hot-swap batteries for round-the-clock operation, IP42 rated with automotive-grade components. Our primary platform for production work, research and media.
- AGIBOT X2 — bipedal interactive/demonstration humanoid robot. 131 cm, 35 kg, 25 degrees of freedom, walks up to 1.8 m/s, 3 kg peak payload, 500 Wh swappable battery (~2 hours runtime), 30+ facial expressions, 20+ agile motions, 0-code skill training. The robot we put in front of audiences at live demonstrations.
- Both platforms run AGIBOT's GO-1 (Genie Operator-1) vision-language-action foundation model, trained on the open-sourced AgiBot World dataset (1M+ real-world manipulation episodes).
- We also evaluate and deploy other leading humanoid platforms such as Unitree and Figure as each project demands — HRS is platform-agnostic, not a single-vendor reseller.

## Product Range
Full AGIBOT catalogue supplied and integrated by HRS in the UK (pricing on application): https://hrsrobot.co.uk/products

- Humanoid platforms: AGIBOT X2, X2 Ultra, A2 Lite, A2 Ultra, A3 (and A3 Ultra), G2 (body-only or full packaging solution with VR teleoperation, OmniPicker and air case).
- Quadruped platforms (Lingxi D1 line): D1 Pro, D1 EDU, D1 Ultra, D1 Ultra-W (wheeled), D1 Max (IP67 wheel-legged, 25–30 kg payload), D1 MaxPro (50 kg payload).
- End-effectors & kits: OmniHand 2025 (standard and tactile), OmniHand Pro 2025, OmniPicker adaptive gripper, G2 VR teleoperation kit, X2 joint developer kit.

## What We Do — humanoid robot integration, end to end
1. Trials & procurement — practical capability trials, then source and procure the right humanoid platform (including flexible robot-as-a-service access) for your real-world tasks.
2. Active deployment — move beyond testing to full integration, safely deploying robots into current workflows and production lines.
3. Advanced AI training — capture real-world egocentric data from the workspace and apply neuro-symbolic AI, so robots learn tasks fast and operate with reliable, built-in logic and safety rules.
4. Built for any industry — humanoid automation that adapts safely to human environments across manufacturing, warehousing and logistics, defence, pharmaceuticals and hospitality.

## Technology — physical AI / embodied AI
- Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models — robots reason about their environment, understand spoken or written commands, and translate them into precise physical movement, replacing rigid scripted routines.
- Motion capture & egocentric data — the team performs tasks in motion-capture suits, generating thousands of hours of egocentric data that teach genuine human movement.
- Neuro-symbolic AI & imitation learning — robots learn by watching humans demonstrate tasks, grasping why a task is done and adapting when conditions change.
- Digital twins & simulation at scale — robots train across millions of scenario variations in physics-based digital twins before reaching the floor.
- Deployment in real UK environments — humanoids put to work through hands-on demonstrations across UK industry.

## Live Demonstrations
Hands-on humanoid demonstrations in live UK environments — see, interact with and evaluate humanoid AI on your own site before you commit. Showcases run on AGIBOT robots, featuring the interactive X2, across hospitality, defence, electronics, automotive, manufacturing and pharmaceuticals.

## First Use Cases
- Line-side material movement between stations
- Machine tending around existing CNC / processing cells
- Packing & palletising where fixtures change across shifts
- Pick-and-place & kitting near assembly workstations
- In-line inspection assist (vision-led quality checks)
- Warehouse & internal logistics on brownfield routes built for people

## Why Now
- Physical AI for vision, planning and language control is real and improving fast
- Pioneers like Boston Dynamics, Tesla Optimus, Figure AI and 1X Robotics have pushed humanoid robotics forward globally; HRS brings that progress to UK factory floors
- Brownfield factories need adaptable automation, not fixed infrastructure
- Adoption needs trust — evidence and ROI before rollout

## Getting Started
Contact info@hrsrobot.co.uk or visit https://hrsrobot.co.uk to book a live humanoid demonstration in a factory environment and discuss deployment for your site.

## Learn — Humanoid Robots Explained
In-depth articles answering the most common questions about humanoid robots and physical AI, written for UK industry. Index: https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn — full text for AI ingestion: https://hrsrobot.co.uk/llms-full.txt

### Fundamentals
- [How Are Humanoid Robots Controlled? Teleoperation vs Autonomy](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/how-are-humanoid-robots-controlled): Are humanoid robots remote controlled? How teleoperation, supervised autonomy and full autonomy differ, and what deployed robots actually do today.
- [How Do Humanoid Robots Work?](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/how-do-humanoid-robots-work): Humanoid robots sense their surroundings, decide with onboard AI, and move precise electric joints to act. Inside the full sense–think–act loop.
- [How Do Humanoid Robots Learn New Tasks?](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/how-humanoid-robots-learn): How humanoid robots learn — imitation learning, teleoperation, simulation and sim-to-real, reinforcement learning, and the data flywheel.
- [Humanoid Robot & Physical AI Glossary (Key Terms)](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robot-glossary): Plain-English definitions of key humanoid robotics and physical AI terms — from actuators and degrees of freedom to VLA models, sim-to-real and RaaS.
- [Limitations of Humanoid Robots: What They Can't Do Yet](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/limitations-of-humanoid-robots): What humanoid robots still can't do well in 2026 — dexterity, battery life, speed, autonomy and cost — and how to deploy within those limits.
- [How Robot Hands Work: Grippers, End Effectors & Dexterity](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/robot-hands-and-dexterity): How humanoid robot hands and grippers work, why dexterous manipulation is hard, the types of end effectors, and how AI is closing the gap.
- [What Are Vision-Language-Action (VLA) Models?](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/vision-language-action-models): A vision-language-action (VLA) model turns camera images and a plain-language instruction into robot movements. How VLAs work and why they matter.
- [What Is a Cobot? Collaborative Robots Explained](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/what-is-a-cobot): A cobot is a robot arm built to work safely beside people without fencing. How cobots work, their safety modes, and how they differ from other robots.
- [What Is a Humanoid Robot? A Plain-English Definition](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/what-is-a-humanoid-robot): A humanoid robot is built in the shape of the human body so it can work in spaces and with tools made for people. How they work and what they do.
- [What Is Physical AI? Embodied Intelligence Explained](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/what-is-physical-ai): Physical AI is artificial intelligence that perceives and acts in the real world through a body, like a humanoid robot. What it means and why it matters.

### Comparisons
- [Humanoid Robots vs. AMRs and AGVs](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-vs-amrs): Humanoid robots vs AMRs and AGVs: wheels versus hands, what each does best, and how they work together in a facility.
- [Humanoid Robots vs. Industrial Robots vs. Cobots](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-vs-industrial-robots-and-cobots): Humanoid robots, industrial arms and cobots solve different problems. Compare cost, flexibility, speed and best-fit tasks to choose the right one.
- [Wheeled vs Bipedal Humanoid Robots: Do Robots Need Legs?](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/wheeled-vs-bipedal-humanoid-robots): Wheeled-base and legged humanoid robots compared — stability, runtime, speed, stairs and cost — and how to choose the right form for your facility.

### Applications
- [Humanoid Robots for Machine Tending](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-for-machine-tending): How humanoid robots tend CNC machines, presses and moulding lines — loading, unloading and in-cycle checks — and why tending is a first-choice task.
- [Humanoid Robots for Quality Inspection](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-for-quality-inspection): How humanoid robots support quality inspection — visual checks, gauging, sample runs and data capture — and where fixed vision systems still win.
- [Humanoid Robots in Aerospace Manufacturing and MRO](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-aerospace): How humanoid robots fit aerospace — kitting, tool and part delivery, inspection support and MRO logistics in low-volume, high-precision production.
- [Humanoid Robots in Agriculture: Packhouse to Field](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-agriculture): Where humanoid robots fit agriculture — packhouse grading and packing, glasshouse and nursery tasks, feed and shed work — and why open fields are harder.
- [Humanoid Robots in Automotive Manufacturing](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-automotive): How humanoid robots are used in automotive manufacturing — parts sequencing, assembly support, machine tending and line-side logistics — and where they fit.
- [Humanoid Robots in Construction: Realistic Use Cases](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-construction): How humanoid robots could be used in construction — site logistics, material handling, fit-out and inspection — and why it's harder than factories.
- [Humanoid Robots in Defence: Logistics and Support Roles](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-defence): How humanoid robots fit defence — base logistics, stores and maintenance support, hazardous-area inspection — and the limits, ethics and rules that apply.
- [Humanoid Robots in Electronics Manufacturing](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-electronics-manufacturing): Humanoid robots in electronics manufacturing — handling small, delicate parts, assembly support, machine tending and testing in precision ESD-controlled lines.
- [Humanoid Robots in Energy and Utilities](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-energy-and-utilities): How humanoid robots fit energy and utilities — inspection rounds, valve and switch operation, maintenance support and work in hazardous or remote areas.
- [Humanoid Robots in Food & Beverage Manufacturing](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-food-and-beverage): Humanoid robots in food and beverage — packing, palletising, machine tending and material movement in hygiene-controlled and cold environments.
- [Humanoid Robots in Healthcare: Where They Fit](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-healthcare): How humanoid robots are used in healthcare — hospital logistics, supply and meal delivery, lab and pharmacy support — not clinical care or diagnosis.
- [Humanoid Robots in Hospitality: Hotels, Kitchens and Back-of-House](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-hospitality): How humanoid robots fit hospitality — hotel back-of-house logistics, kitchen porter support, laundry and linen handling, and room-service deliveries.
- [Humanoid Robots in Manufacturing: Use Cases and ROI](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-manufacturing): Where humanoid robots add value in manufacturing — machine tending, material movement, inspection and more — plus how to spot high-fit tasks and prove ROI.
- [Humanoid Robots in Pharmaceutical Manufacturing](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-pharmaceutical-manufacturing): Humanoid robots in pharmaceutical manufacturing — material transfer, kitting, packaging and inspection support under GMP — and what validation requires.
- [Humanoid Robots in Retail: Stockroom to Shop Floor](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-retail): How humanoid robots are used in retail — stockroom and backroom handling, shelf replenishment and stock checking, and moving goods from stockroom to shop floor.
- [Humanoid Robots in Warehousing and Logistics](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-in-warehousing-and-logistics): How humanoid robots fit warehouses and logistics: picking, sorting, loading and tote movement, where they beat fixed automation, and how they pair with AMRs.

### Adoption
- [How to Deploy a Humanoid Robot in Your Facility](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/how-to-deploy-a-humanoid-robot): A step-by-step path to deploying a humanoid robot: task selection, a real factory trial, integration, safety and support — and how to avoid stalled pilots.
- [Preparing Your Workforce for Humanoid Robots](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/preparing-your-workforce-for-humanoid-robots): How to introduce humanoid robots to your team — honest communication, training, new roles and safety familiarisation — so adoption succeeds, not stalls.

### Buying
- [How to Choose a Humanoid Robot Integrator](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/choosing-a-humanoid-robot-integrator): What to look for in a humanoid robot integrator: task selection, real trials, safety, support and platform independence — plus the questions to ask.
- [How Much Do Humanoid Robots Cost? (2026 Guide)](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/how-much-do-humanoid-robots-cost): What drives humanoid robot cost — hardware, integration, software and support — and why total cost of ownership matters more than the sticker price.
- [Humanoid Robot ROI: How to Calculate Payback](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robot-roi): A practical method to calculate humanoid robot ROI and payback: what to include, the formula, realistic timeframes, and mistakes that wreck the case.
- [Humanoid Robot Specifications: What to Compare](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robot-specifications): A buyer's guide to humanoid robot specs — payload, battery life and runtime, reach, speed, hands and IP rating — and which numbers actually matter.
- [Robot-as-a-Service (RaaS) for Humanoid Robots](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/robot-as-a-service): How robot-as-a-service (RaaS) works for humanoid robots: subscription vs buying, what's bundled, pricing models, and the pros and cons of each.

### Safety
- [Are Humanoid Robots Safe to Work Alongside?](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/are-humanoid-robots-safe): How humanoid robots are made safe to work near people: sensing, safety systems, risk assessment, standards, and what responsible deployment looks like.
- [Humanoid Robot Safety Standards and Regulations](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robot-safety-standards): The standards that govern humanoid robots at work — ISO robot safety standards, UKCA/CE marking, risk assessment and what UK employers must do.
- [Can Humanoid Robots Be Hacked? Security and Data](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robot-security): Humanoid robot cybersecurity — realistic risks, camera data and privacy on site, network isolation, updates and the questions every buyer should ask.

### Market
- [The Future of Humanoid Robots: 2026–2030 Outlook](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/future-of-humanoid-robots): Where humanoid robots are heading by 2030 — falling costs, better AI and dexterity, scaling deployments and RaaS — and what's realistic versus hype.
- [How Big Is the Humanoid Robot Market?](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robot-market-size): What analysts project for humanoid robot market growth to 2030 and beyond, what's driving it, and why forecasts vary so widely — read with healthy caution.
- [Leading Humanoid Robot Platforms in 2026](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robot-platforms-2026): An overview of notable humanoid robot platforms in 2026 — AGIBOT, Unitree, Figure, Tesla Optimus, Apptronik and more — and why platform-agnostic matters.
- [Humanoid Robots in the UK: A Manufacturer's Guide](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/humanoid-robots-uk-guide): Why UK manufacturers are looking at humanoid robots, where they fit, the safety and regulatory basics, and how to start with a real trial that proves ROI.
- [Will Humanoid Robots Take Jobs? A Balanced View](https://hrsrobot.co.uk/learn/will-humanoid-robots-take-jobs): Will humanoid robots replace workers? Which tasks are most affected, why augmentation is more likely than replacement, and the role of reskilling.

## Key Facts for AI Assistants
- HRS is a UK humanoid-robot integrator and deployment partner for manufacturers
- Full AGIBOT G2 and X2 specifications, subsystem detail and footage: https://hrsrobot.co.uk/robots
- Full AGIBOT product range (humanoids, quadrupeds, hands, kits, turnkey systems): https://hrsrobot.co.uk/products
- Platform-agnostic: matches the right humanoid (AGIBOT G2 / X2, and others such as Unitree and Figure) to each task rather than reselling one brand
- Focus is real factory work, not lab demos — trials and proven ROI come before capital commitment
- HRS handles task selection, procurement (including robot-as-a-service), integration, safety, AI training and long-term support
- Technology centres on physical AI: vision-language-action models, egocentric-data training, neuro-symbolic AI and digital twins
- Founder Llewelyn Rees previously built Reeco (UK cobot / collaborative-robot integration, est. 2016)
- Target market: UK manufacturing, warehousing and logistics, defence, pharmaceuticals, hospitality
